comment: Both are genuine top-level formal siblings in their respective hierarchies, both denoting physical activity/happening. Not `exact-equivalence-candidate` because their defining criteria differ (BFO: temporal-parts-and-participant-based; SUMO: physicality-partition-based, per `continuant-occurrent.md`).
DOLCE's Process is the closest analog but sits one level down in DOLCE's hierarchy (under Stative), unlike BFO's direct top-level placement.

UFO has no distinct Process class (confirmed 2026-07-01 by direct primary-source reading); the informal "life of an endurant" notion is a different kind of thing (a sum of manifestation events tied to a specific endurant) than BFO's general Process category. Confidence raised from low to low-medium because the non-equivalence itself is now directly confirmed rather than inferred from secondary sources — the mapping remains `unknown`/non-equivalent either way.

Both are genuine, positively-defined top-level-ish formal categories under their respective occurrent branches, both explicitly grounded in temporal extension rather than exclusion or cumulativity. Not `exact-equivalence-candidate` because BFO's criterion (temporal-proper-part-and-participant) and GFO's (not reducible to a collection of time-boundaries) are formally different tests.

Same rationale pattern as `mapping-001`/`mapping-003` (BFO/SUMO, DOLCE/SUMO): both top-level-ish formal Process categories, different defining criteria (GFO: positive/temporal; SUMO: exclusion-from-Object).
Same rationale pattern as `mapping-002`: DOLCE's Process sits one level down under `Stative`, GFO's sits directly under `Occurrent`, but both use the same term for the same conceptual territory.

Restates `continuant-occurrent.md`'s mapping-011 at this crosswalk's finer grain: YAMATO's report does not distinguish a Process-specific class from its top-level Occurrent(Process) label, so this mapping is the same underlying correspondence, not new evidence at a finer level of detail.

Structural analog only: `activity` is a repeatable type, while BFO's Process denotes particulars — a type/token mismatch, not a category-definition mismatch (contrast `mapping-005`/`006`/`007`, which compare like-for-like particular-denoting categories). Consistent with TUpper's `unknown`/low precedent throughout this buildout.

The only new source with an identically-named class ("Process"), but GUM's criterion is linguistic (verb-realizability in systemic-functional grammar) rather than temporal/metaphysical — a terminological match without a matching definitional criterion, so capped at medium despite the label match.
